question: In the context of the Tropical Abel-Jacobi Map's relation to tropical curves, how is the matrix representation QG enabled by an Orthogonal Projection Map?

options.A: It utilizes the incidence matrix I under a dual basis to the space of cycles in the graph G.
options.B: It is determined by the vertex adjacency matrix under the non-standard basis {v : v ∈ V(G)}.
options.C: It employs the Laplacian matrix of the graph G under a spanning tree basis.
options.D: It is represented by the edge length matrix L under the standard basis {e : e ∈ E(G)}.

explanation: The correct answer is A, as the context specifies that under the standard basis {e : e ∈ E(G)}, the matrix representing QG is precisely the edge length matrix L. This represents the matrix form of the Orthogonal Projection Map that enables the Tropical Abel-Jacobi Map. Options B, C, and D mention other matrices and bases not related to the orthogonal projection described in the section.
context: The Tropical Abel–Jacobi Map as an Orthogonal Projection. | The Tropical Abel–Jacobi Map as an Orthogonal Projection. | Under the standard basis {e : e ∈ E(G)}, the matrix representing QG is precisely the edge length matrix L.
